questions.question: According to the outline, what is the core issue of the American Empire?
options: Its reliance on European aristocratic models
Disproportionate use of force due to mass democracy
Lack of nuclear capability until the Cold War
Refusal to join the League of Nations
questions.answer: Disproportionate use of force due to mass democracy

questions.question: What does "hold the metal stick and breathe" represent in the material?
options: A military drill used in World War I
Trust the moment and zoom out from era-specific assumptions to stay sane
A metaphor for industrial production in the American empire
Churchill's strategy for cooperating with the United States
questions.answer: Trust the moment and zoom out from era-specific assumptions to stay sane

questions.question: Why are Rome, Britain, and America described as "reluctant" empires?
options: They were forced to empire by treaty obligations
Their cultures were so dynamic they filled the void without deliberately seeking empire
They lacked aristocratic leadership
They lost wars that expanded their territory
questions.answer: Their cultures were so dynamic they filled the void without deliberately seeking empire

questions.question: How does dollar diplomacy function as tribute in the Pax Americana?
options: The U.S. pays allies to hold dollars
Countries that hold and support the U.S. dollar get American military protection; the U.S. can export inflation
The dollar is backed by gold held in allied nations
Allies send raw materials in exchange for dollar loans
questions.answer: Countries that hold and support the U.S. dollar get American military protection; the U.S. can export inflation

questions.question: What is the "industrial priest class" in this framework?
options: Religious leaders who blessed imperial conquests
Elites trained in set institutions whose herd mechanism enforces conformity
Scientists who developed nuclear weapons in Britain and America
The League of Nations secretariat
questions.answer: Elites trained in set institutions whose herd mechanism enforces conformity

questions.question: Why did the outline claim America could not apply force in the "clinical" way European colonial empires did?
options: America had weaker military technology
Mass-appeal democracy ties foreign policy to voters and prevents calibrated, aristocratic use of force
European empires had more territory
The U.S. Constitution forbade overseas interventions
questions.answer: Mass-appeal democracy ties foreign policy to voters and prevents calibrated, aristocratic use of force

questions.question: What role did the Zimmerman telegram and the Lusitania play in U.S. entry into World War I?
options: They proved German victory was imminent
They served as PR events that turned American public opinion toward entering the war
They triggered a formal alliance with Britain
They led to the creation of the League of Nations
questions.answer: They served as PR events that turned American public opinion toward entering the war

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the transition from Pax Britannica to Pax Americana?
options: A violent war between Britain and America
A nearly seamless transfer of finance, nuclear work, and code-breaking from London to New York
A Soviet-brokered agreement at Yalta
A gradual shift that took place only after the Suez crisis
questions.answer: A nearly seamless transfer of finance, nuclear work, and code-breaking from London to New York

questions.question: Modernity is described in the outline as an alliance of which two tendencies?
options: The aristocratic and the democratic
The hysterical feminine and the autistic masculine
The imperial and the anti-imperial
The industrial and the agrarian
questions.answer: The hysterical feminine and the autistic masculine

questions.question: What was the "core issue" thesis about America and global leadership?
options: America had too much aristocracy
America took global leadership before developing the aristocratic or civilizational traits Europe had used to govern
Europe transferred its institutions too late
The Cold War prevented America from developing those traits
questions.answer: America took global leadership before developing the aristocratic or civilizational traits Europe had used to govern

questions.question: Why did America create the League of Nations but not join it?
options: Wilson died before ratification
Republicans won in a landslide and refused to join the organization Wilson had championed
The British and French vetoed U.S. membership
The Senate required a two-thirds majority that Wilson could not obtain
questions.answer: Republicans won in a landslide and refused to join the organization Wilson had championed

questions.question: According to Mackinder's thesis, what did "heartland" refer to?
options: The American Midwest
The Eurasian interior (former Mongol Empire); controlling it was thought to mean ruling the world
The British Isles
The Pacific Rim
questions.answer: The Eurasian interior (former Mongol Empire); controlling it was thought to mean ruling the world

questions.question: In the outline, what is the "universal person" (from the Hermetica)?
options: A single world leader proposed by Wilson
The bedrock human nature that persists across eras when context is stripped away
The ideal citizen of the League of Nations
A character in Roman imperial propaganda
questions.answer: The bedrock human nature that persists across eras when context is stripped away

questions.question: Why is the Pax Americana called an "anti-empire empire"?
options: It never conquered any territory
It carries out imperial functions while officially opposing empire
It was defeated by the Soviet Union
It gave up all military bases after World War II
questions.answer: It carries out imperial functions while officially opposing empire

questions.question: What is said to have happened to Congress's war power after World War II?
options: It was strengthened by the League of Nations
Congress signed it away; the president could wage "police actions" without formal declarations; no war declared since 1942
It was transferred to the United Nations
It was shared equally with the Supreme Court
questions.answer: Congress signed it away; the president could wage "police actions" without formal declarations; no war declared since 1942

questions.question: How did the outline explain imperial elites in Rome, Britain, and America?
options: They always put their home population first
They cooperated with conquered or external peoples to secure power over their own country; imperial institutions turned on their own people
They refused to use local aristocracies
They disbanded their empires voluntarily
questions.answer: They cooperated with conquered or external peoples to secure power over their own country; imperial institutions turned on their own people

questions.question: What did national self-determination at Versailles (for Wilson) apply to?
options: All peoples equally
Only white people
Only the defeated Central Powers
Only British and French colonies

questions.question: Why did the outline cite Vietnam as an example of American failure?
options: America did not have enough troops
America should have assessed value and applied calibrated force; instead it encouraged decolonization, left a void, drafted youth, and waffled between conquest and withdrawal
The Soviet Union had superior technology
Congress declared war too late
questions.answer: America should have assessed value and applied calibrated force; instead it encouraged decolonization, left a void, drafted youth, and waffled between conquest and withdrawal
